release.title: Profound Sounds Vol. 1
release.notes:
Track 1 title is given as "D2" (which is the side of record on the vinyl version of i220-010 release). This was also released on CD where this track is listed on 8th position. On both version no titles are given (only writing/producing credits). You can see both versions of i220-010 at the master release page [m27265]. Track 1 and 12 contains acapella of "Sixth Sense" by Josh Wink featuring Ursula Rucker. Track 4 is credited as "J. Dahlbäck". Have been released later under "The Persuader". Track 5 is wrongly titled as Vol. 1. Track 6 is credited as "Gez Varley presents Tony Montana". Track 8 has the first artist misspelled as "Merio's Dubwork". Track 10 is credited as "Stacey Pullen presents Black Odyssey". Track 12 is exclusive to this release.
Artist and track names are typed in all lower case.
Some copies released with hype sticker K 63628 S1 on jewelcase lid.

release.title: Uncut

artist.name: Don Grossinger
artist.role: Mastered By [DMM, Runout Etching Treble Clef]

release.notes:
Published By U...sic Publishing. (P)+(C) 1999 UC... UCMG NY ny@ucmg.com
DMM : Direct Metal Mastering
The track B2 is a break. Blue side is the side A. On the run-outs, ‘1’ are etched ‘I’, the third ‘0’ of ‘0001’, etched on the side B, is crossed out. The release was originally issued in a semi-glossy black die-cut sleeve, including a white die-cut inner-sleeve with cut corners.

release.title: The Collected Works Of Kosmic Messenger
release.notes:
electronic//Poetry all/tracks/written/performed/produced/ /for/ / Whallha Productions published/by// Whalla Music/ /BMI licensed/from//Black Flag Recordings/ /Detroit eye to eye/deathmarch/flash/ /licensed/from/ /Plink Plonk Records Elypsia Records (logo) / Black Flag Recordings (logo)
℗+© 1997/ /Future Sound Productions s.a
thank you//Everyone
The release was originally issued in a matte gatefold sleeve with the left spine printed. The print is using a 5th silvered colour. It includes two white innersleeve with the corners largely cut and a hole on one side only. The records are 150 grams in weight.
Erroneously listed ELY 014 CD on back cover (under barcode) On the sleeve tracks are listed erroneously like this: A1 Get Down A2 Fullmoon B1 Eye To Eye B2 Flash B3 Memories Of You C1 I Find Myself C2 Freeky Deeky C3 Kosmic Funk D1 The Deathmarch D2 Alley Cat

Limited to 3000 copies. Direct Metal Mastering (DMM).

Several titles are anagrams: Hangable Auto Bulb = Analogue Bub[b]l[e]bath Laughable Butane Bob = Analogue Bubblebath Custodian Discount = Caussttic Ouinnddo [Caustic Window]
Issued in a generic glossy white outer sleeve. Some track times printed on the labels are different to the actual durations.

release.title: Anti EP
release.notes:
Warp Music/EMI Music. ℗ & © 1994 Warp Records. Made in England.
Printed on sticker placed as a seal: "Warning: Lost and Djarum contain repetitive beats. We advise you not to play these tracks if the Criminal Justice Bill becomes law. Flutter has been programmed in such a way that no bars contain identical beats and can therefore be played at 45 or 33 revolutions under the proposed law. However we advise DJs to have a lawyer and musicologist present at all times to confirm the non-repetitive nature of the music in the event of police harassment.
Important: By breaking this seal, you accept full responsibility for any consequential action resulting from the product's use, as playing the music contained within these recordings may be interpreted as opposition to the Criminal Justice and Public Order Bill."
Although the note above implies that 'Flutter' can be played at either speed, the track on the CD release correlates to the 45 rpm version. The A side plays at 33⅓ rpm.

release.title: Anti EP
release.notes:
AeP Agitate.educate.Protest.
Printed on sticker placed as a seal:
Warning. Lost and Djarum contain repetitive beats. We advise you not to play these tracks if the Criminal Justice Bill becomes law. Flutter has been programmed in such a way that no bars contain identical beats and can therefore be played at both forty five and thirty three revolutions under the proposed new law. However we advise DJs to have a lawyer and musicologist present at all times to confirm the non repetitive nature of the music in the event of police harrassment.
Important. By breaking this seal, you accept full responsibilty for any consequential action resulting from the product's use, as playing the music contained within these recordings may be interpreted as oppostion to the Criminal Justice and Public Order Bill.
On the back of the sticker is a declaration that 'all profits from this release will be donated to Liberty'. A brief history and mission statement for Liberty (The National Council for Civil Liberties) is also given, followed by contact details. This ends with a statement stating that 'Autechre is politically non-aligned. This is about personal freedom'.
℗&© 1994 Warp Records. All rights reserved. Made in England

release.title: Key Nell
release.notes:
Comes packaged in a custom bubble-wrap sleeve instead of a regular record sleeve. Pressed at Orlake. Both sides end-up in locked grooves.
Known versions: First pressing (this release), side E in white w/ black and side F in black w/white. [url=http://www.discogs.com/release/1186445]Repress[/url], side E in black w/ white and side F in white w/ black.
Tracks E2 and F2 are slightly longer than those found on the [url=http://www.discogs.com/Gescom-Key-Nell/release/65711]CD reissue[/url].

release.title: Keynell / Keynell
release.notes:
Autechre remixes of Gescom's [url=https://www.discogs.com/Gescom-Key-Nell/master/18519]Key Nell[/url] release (SKA007) on Skam Records.
Originally ([url=https://www.discogs.com/Autechre-Keynell/release/1165003]promo 12" release[/url]) credited as an Autechre release. Printed on far left of spine reads 'Ae' where artist credits are normally found. Spine also notes: Twelve" only.
Original sleeves have additional text & artwork printed on the inside of the sleeve.
Text printed on inside spine: "if you've got this far you're a sad trainspotter keynell pt. 2 (moto yori & openmind) design from the inside"
Published WARP/EMI Music ℗ WARP Records 1996 © WARP Records 1996

release.title: Hi Scores
release.notes:
©skam recordz inc. 1996
This is the first pressing. Characteristics for this pressing are: - back sleeve has a clear sticker affixed (under "skam") with BOARDS OF CANADA on it in braille. - back sleeve has a barcode: 5 021904051061 - centre labels: blue text on white background - centre labels: written + produced by mike + marcus - centre labels: made in england. - centre labels: no "recorded at hexagon sun 1996" printed. - insert: A4 on blue (cyan) paper with black printing, showing photographs where faces are in black and the background in blue Some copies also had a 2nd insert: a Skam Information A4 on white paper with black printing, containg the text "No information at this moment in time".
The [url=http://www.discogs.com/release/1177164]second pressing[/url] from 1999 also has the sticker but does not have the barcode on the back. The [url=http://www.discogs.com/release/1182252]third and fourth pressings[/url] from 2002 and 2006 have stamped braille instead of a sticker on the sleeve and contain the barcode again.

release.title: Tranquilizer EP
release.notes:
Mastered at National Sound Corporation, Detroit. All tracks produced and arranged for Axis. Published by Millsart (BMI) Detox (BMI)
Labels wrongly list side A with a single track "Mutant Theory" and side B with 2 tracks "Sleepchamber" and "88". In fact "Mutant Theory" is a single track on side B and side A contains the other 2 tracks.
Side B plays from the inside out.
This is the original release with black/beige label art, the repress has black/white labels.
Translucent vinyl copies exist.

release.title: Chinese Whispers
release.notes:
© Sprawl Imprint 1998 ℗ Sprawl Imprint 1998
Made in England.
The remix album with no composer...the track with no name. Unlike other remix albums Chinese Whispers is a hermetically sealed cyclical recording remix project, but with no original track to begin with. All the artists participating had no idea who they were remixing. They were working in the dark, samples arriving anonymously. All they were aware of was who was involved, who these audio snippets might be from. Stereolab provided the original unconnected miscellaneous samples (ie not from an existing track) to start the ball rolling, and nine remixes later were given the final samples from the last artist's track (in this case T Power) back, to create their final track.
Packaging: standard plastic jewel box case, clear tray, 6 page fold-out cover booklet.
Track times not printed on sleeve art.
Total time: 58:13
Track 1 published by Island Publishing Ltd/Copyright Control. Track 2, 5 to 8 Copyright Control. Track 3 Copyright Control/published by Chrysalis Music Ltd. Track 4 published by Chrysalis Music Ltd/Copyright Control. Track 9 Copyright Control/published by Mutesong. Track 10 published by Mutesong/Island Publishing.

release.title: Compilation
release.notes:
A full (slower) version of the drone track that can just about be heard in the mix of The Garden Of Linmiri can be found on the AFX - [m=919] CD release as CD Track 11.
CAUSTIC WINDOW Compilation JOYREX J4 + JOYREX J5 + JOYREX J9 E.P's 13 classic Braindance™ tunes from Cornwall. Formerly available on Vinyl only... now on CD! Proudly presented by REPHLEX (CAT 009 CD) Pub'd by Chrysalis Music. Made in England. REPHLEX, POB 2676, London N11. (0)181 361 2811. www.rephlex.com CAUSTIC WINDOW = RICHARD D. JAMES (Richard made in Ireland.) Also available as AFX / Polygon Window etc. Proudly presented by The James Family, e.g :-
Distributed in the UK by Vital (0)181 324 2400 ℗ © 1993 Rephlex
Issued in a jewel case with clear tray and a fold-out insert including label catalogue listing (winter 1991-spring 1998). Technically there is a slight misprint with the catalogue listing, in that the release after CAT 002 was CAT 007 (the artist (Mike Dred) was a James Bond fan and requested the number instead of CAT 003). The listing has chosen to move that album to after CAT 006 instead. But then a lot of listings will be out of order anyway because Rephlex had a habit of releasing some albums out of order, even to the point of giving out fake release dates after the fact (someone in 1996 asking about exactly when a 1993 album came out would get the original anticipated release date instead of the date it actually released on). EFA distribution code only to be found on sticker (back cover).

release.title: Hmm
release.notes:
Hmm is about comparing the structures and functions of early sacred music with electronic or digital musics.
Hmm is about exploring parallels, re-visiting the sounds and messages we were issued with as children, restructuring those memory fragments. In re-investigating the past we can re-invent the sonic present and future.
Mastered at Wolf Studios - many thanks to Dominic Thanks to Rob Young and Robin Rimbaud Special thanks to the artists, and the composers, living and dead

release.title: Solar: A Music Travelogue Volume 2
release.notes:
"Solar is a series of music travelogues (a journal in sound) charting the ever changing cross cultural currents in music's textural paths. Electroacoustics, sound sculptors, field recordings, traditional percussion, self built instruments, fourth world and electronic experimentation, genres and distinctions have become blurred reference points. This edition features over 58 minutes of exclusive and specially recorded material and includes an 18 panel guide combining information on the contributors with captured images."
Track 5 - live location Binarual recording - single take - no rehearsal; improvisation using found materials, recorded at Colwick Park, Nottingham 17/3/97 Track 6 - recorded at Westheath Studios, London
Manufactured in the USA.

release.title: Penguin Mechanics Vol III
release.notes:
Original copies came with various machine parts placed inside the spine of the jewel case.
The mechanics were given nearly complete freedom, with the rough guideline of constructing a track that resembles the machine; possibly combining recorded and synthesized sounds of -- klankgrinding, motor whirring, metallic collisions, beeps and buzzes -- into rhythmic components.

release.title: Lowercase
release.notes:
Discs 3 and 4 are copies of 1 and 2, to "please pass on to another curious listener." Also includes 30 index cards: one for each of the CD tracks, containing biographical info on the musicians involved, and three with illustrations. Everything is packaged in a special paper box.
1-2 composed in Studios C&C (Antwerp) and Crfmw (Liege), 1998, Belgium. 1-3 recording, editing, additional sounds, mixing, mastering at trente oiseaux studio, 1999. 1-4 formed + recorded, August 1999. 1-5 is credited to Nosei Sakata (*0). 1-7 recorded Summer 1999 at Intransitive; based on a performance at WMFO, Tufts University Radio; dedicated to James Coleman and Richard Chartier. 1-8 excerpt from 43 min work; first presented as an installation at the ASU Art Museum Tempe AZ, 1999. 1-9 played live with Zoom Sampletrack ST-224 with prerecorded samples of laboratory equipment recorded with a high-gain surveillance contact microphone; dedicated to Sandy, who made it all possible. 1-11 thanks to Trey Jones for use of his computer. 1-12 small speakers emitting wire drones and incidental movements recorded in a mine shaft located near Kingston, New Mexico; mastered at the Watershed, Austin, Texas; thanks to Joe and Suzanne for the invitation to the Kingston dome. 2-1 constructed with a combination of analog modular synthesizer and synthesis software on a Macintosh G3. 2-3 conceived at night, executed by day… 2-5 made with a guitar and a Mac 3beam, September 1999. 2-6 made over a two day period using a Macintosh G3 with Cubase software, one Digetec Quad 2 and a cheap Walkman. 2-7 recorded live for the 90.3 FM Sonarchy radio broadcast at the Jack Straw Foundation studios, Seattle, WA. 2-10 recorded on the night of 08/20-21/1999. 2-13 created with tracking software/special process.

Track 10 title listed as "Free" on back, as "Free At Last" in booklet.

Tracks 1 and 4 produced and recorded at Fishtank Recording Studio, Melbourne. Tracks 2 and 3 regenerated and reef fried for Lords Garden Productions. Taken from the album [m=67100].

track.title: Addicted To Bass (Lords Garden Dub Buttons Mix)
Addicted To Bass (Lords Garden Reef Fried Mix)
En Voyage (1965 Melbourne University Language Dept. Mix)

video.title: JOSH ABRAHAMS and AMIEL DAEMION - Addicted To Bass (1998)
video.description:
"Addicted to Bass" is a single by Puretone, featuring Amiel Daemion on vocals. It reached the top 20 of the Australian charts in 1998 and #2 in the UK in 2002. In the US, "addicted to Bass" went to number one on the dance charts.
The original 1998 music

video.title: Josh Abrahams & Amiel Daemion - Addicted To Bass (Original Mix)
video.description:
The most easily found of anything on this single, though I must admit, my least favourite track of the 4. It's great, but I just get along with the remixes better.
This is Track 1 (of 4)

video.title: Josh Abrahams, The Lord's Garden - Addicted To Bass (Lords Garden Dub Buttons Mix)
video.description: The 1st of the 2 Lord's Garden remixes. As a big fan of dub, I like this one quite a bit. This is Track 2 (of 4)

video.title: Josh Abrahams, The Lord's Garden - Addicted To Bass (Lords Garden Reef Fried Mix)
video.description:
2nd of the 2 Lord's Garden remixes, and I really like the groovy vibe they brought to this one.
This is Track 3 (of 4)

video.title: Josh Abrahams - En Voyage (1965 Melbourne University Language Dept. Mix)
video.description: The reason I sought out this release in the first place. A very interesting name, but thankfully it wasn't a let-down. I think it's my favourite of the 4. I like the vibe, it's very chill. The percussion is nice too. This is Track 4 (of 4)

release.title: Closer Colder
release.notes:
Released in a glossy six panel Digipak with transparent tray.
Physical track 8 is 18:17 long but the audio ends at 5:11 and the remainder of the track is silence. Track 9 is not mentioned on the sleeve. It's titled "Home" on [url=https://www.discogs.com/release/277299]this compilation[/url].
Voices from an unknown Nuisance Caller are credited on track 5, and from the sex phone service Worldwide Partyline on 8. Pianos recorded at Master Rock Studios, London.

release.notes:

B2 is a re-work of a track of the same name from the album [url=http://www.discogs.com/release/223609]Another Domestic Drama In A Suburban Hell[/url] by one of Bernd's former projects called [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Some+More+Crime]Some More Crime[/url]. release.title: Occer
release.notes:
This is the first pressing of 500 copies on clear vinyl.
There was a [url=https://www.discogs.com/Arovane-Occer/release/2999106]repress[/url] of 350 copies on black vinyl in April 1999.
Notes from a slip of paper enclosed in the sleeve:
"silicad
es verwandelt eine silbrig graue linie in strahlend tiefes blau. so schnell wie licht. es gibt kein zuruck. es gibt nur ein hindurch.
occer
eine melodie die du im herzen tragst, schenk sie einem freund, bring sie auf die spitze des hochsten berges, leg sie auf die wolken, lass sie treiben, und schau ihr nach, bis sie am horizont verschwindet."
The record also comes with 2 sets of 8 tiny stickers. Their texts vary: "Wing Fat Supermarket", "Kaisers Kaffee Markt", "Tesco", "Tip Discount", "KaDeWe", "Kwik Save" etc.

release.title: Systems Medley / Planets
release.notes:
Side one is 45 rpm, but side 2 is 33.3 or variable.
First print: 750 copies (January 2000) [this] [url=http://www.discogs.com/release/3529050]Second print[/url]: 300 copies (February 2000) [url=http://www.discogs.com/Snd-Systems-Medley-Planets/release/3529041]Third print[/url]: 500 copies (July 2000).
Released in a black paper sleeve with sticker on it featuring all release information.
Initial copies also came with 8 little white stickers that say 'Rehberge' and 8 that say 'Fletcher Moss'. On other copies 8 stickers say 'Rehgebirge' and 8 that say 'Heaton Park'
Although there are actually 9 locked grooves, the label on side B says "8 Locked Grooves". This was a mistake from the record label's side. On the later pressings, the sticker on the cover reads "Planets" instead of "8 Locked Grooves". Each locked groove represents a planet in the solar system.

release.title: Adventures In Foam
release.notes:
Courtesy of NINEBARecords.
The original version of the release is housed in a brown cardboard, tri-fold slipcase with the white CD inserted in the center. The different album art was purportedly not approved by Amon Tobin.
Track 11 is incorrectly listed as "The Sequel," but is actually titled "Clockwork."
Track 14 contains a hidden, untitled track that begins at 8:18, after 2:27 of silence. This version of this track is shorter than the version on [url=http://www.discogs.com/release/32615]the original NINEBARecords album[/url], but only due to excess empty space being omitted.
In addition to these two changes, the track list is reorganized from the original NINEBARecords version, omitting "Ol' Bunkhouse," "The Method," and "On The Track." These tracks were replaced with "The Light," "The Brazilianaire," and "Clockwork." Of the three different songs, only "The Brazilionaire" was previously released, on NINEBARecords' [url=http://www.discogs.com/release/176049]On The Track[/url] 12".
Album runtime - 73:40
Late 1990s/early2000s press

release.title: Expert Knob Twiddlers
release.notes:
The cover is a parody of the box cover of "Downfall", a board game by Milton Bradley.
Fans of the album, record stores, and Planet Mu's official site state that the title of this release is "Expert Knob Twiddlers". The Planet Mu site says : "… Mike & Rich’s 'Expert Knob Twiddlers' was made back in 1994. Richard edited the tracks into shape later in 1996 with his new Apple Mac computer and it was released later that year on Rephlex …". This text is not an instance of retrofitting; 'Expert Knob Twiddlers' was the title upon the original release in 1996. The cover of the [url=http://www.discogs.com/release/892016]Japanese CD version[/url] and the catalogue summaries included inside [url=http://www.discogs.com/release/296]Caustic Window "Compilation"[/url] and [r=20737] list this release by the artist name only.
℗&© 1996 rephlex. Made in England.
Cat# appears as "cat 027cd" on spine, back, in fold out insert and on disk (big text). Cat# appears as "cat 027lp" on disk (small text).
Issued in a jewel case with clear tray and an 8 page fold-out insert.
Black round sticker on front stating: "CAT 027 LP / CD / MC REFLEX presents MIKE & RICH Mr. MIKE PARADINAS (aka µ-ZIQ) & Mr. RICHARD JAMES (aka AFX) You can remove this sticker if you buy this right now."
Some copies have EFA distribution code on sticker (rear).

identifier.value: CAT027CD 10420191 01 % MADE IN U.K. BY PDO

release.title: Soulmate
release.notes:
The record itself and jacket are mislabeled. The second track on side A is actually the "Soulmate (Naked Music MAJ Original)" mix, while the second track on side B is actually "Soulmate (Swag Remix)". The above tracklist is the actual one.
Track A1 (Restless Soul Peaktime Vocal Mix): remix and arrangement for Restless Soul Productions. Mixed and engineered at Tony's. Track A2 (Naked Music MAJ Original): produced for Naked Music NYC Recordings, LLC. Track B1 (Mig's Petalpusher Bump): engineered at Moulton Street Studios/SF. Track B2 (Swag Remix): remix and arrangement for Swag Productions.
c & p Diastolic Music (ASCAP) / Aquarian Outcast Music (BMI) 1999.
